Benzene prices trend lower in Europe
data.description: Last week, benzene prices decreased in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, " European benzene and styrene prices have decreased due to rising trade tensions and uncertainty regarding tariffs. The market exhibited a contango structure between April and May, characterized by narrower bid-offer ranges and further declines in upstream markets. Styrene prices fell more sharply than those of benzene, a trend attributed to both U.S. tariffs and the influx of import volumes into Europe. Daily benzene prices declined, influenced by lower price expectations and the forecasted arrival of additional imports in late April. Previous reductions in benzene prices were associated with a significant drop in crude oil and gasoline prices."The source added, "Concurrently, benzene prices continued to decline steadily on a day-to-day basis, driven by muted pricing sentiments and expectations of increased supply. The anticipated influx of import volumes in the latter half of April may worsen the existing oversupply situation. The recent downturn in benzene pricing mirrors earlier declines, primarily linked to a fall in crude oil and gasoline prices. With upstream energy markets remaining unstable, the future for aromatics such as benzene and styrene is uncertain. Industry experts indicate that unless there is a resurgence in demand or greater geopolitical stability, European petrochemical markets may continue to experience downward pricing pressure in the short term. Market participants will be monitoring tariff changes, global trade dynamics, and inventory levels for further guidance. On Friday, FOB Rotterdam benzene prices were assessed at the USD 670-680/mt levels, a week on week fall of USD (-10/mt).Downstream SM prices on Friday were assessed at the USD 1315-1325/mt FOB Rotterdam levels, a tumble of USD (-85/mt) from last week.Meanwhile in Asia, benzene prices on Friday were assessed at the USD 735-745/mt FOB Korea levels, sharply lower by USD (-45/mt) from the previous week.European benzene contract price for April 2025 has been settled at the Euro 669/MT CIF North West Europe. This price represents a steep drop of Euro 179/MT from its March 2025 settlement levels.

data.title: M&T Bank Q1 Non-GAAP Earnings, Revenue Increase
data.description:
M&T Bank reported Q1 non-GAAP operating earnings Monday of $3.38 per diluted share, up from $3.09 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $3.40.

Revenue for the quarter ended March 31, expressed as the sum of net interest income and total noninterest income, was $2.32 billion, up from $2.26 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $2.35 billion.

Shares of the company were down more than 2% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Market Chatter: SenseTime Rolls Out SenseNova V6, V6 Reasoner Models
data.description:
SenseTime introduced its SenseNova V6 and V6 Reasoner models, which are new iterations of its self-developed artificial intelligence model series, the South China Morning Post reported Friday.

The V6, which has 600 billion parameters, is said to have outperformed OpenAI's GPT-4o in several aspects, including fact-checking, numerical reasoning, data analysis, and visualization, the report said, citing SenseTime Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Xu Li.

SenseTime's new developments are designed to address the depletion of high-quality text data for training large language models, according to the SCMP.

SenseNova is still closed-source, as Xu said the current motivation for being closed-source was to drive commercialization, the report said.

However, SenseNova might consider making the models open-source once they could provide a unique value to the industry, according to the SCMP, citing Xu.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: HCL Technologies Secures Three Google Cloud Partner Specializations
data.description:
HCL Technologies said it has received three new Google Cloud Partner Specializations under the Google Partner Advantage program, according to a Friday filing.

These include Generative AI Services, Cloud Migration Services, and Infrastructure Modernization Services, bringing its total to seven specializations, the filing said.

The Generative AI Services specialization was awarded for work using tools such as Google Vertex AI and the Responsible Generative AI toolkit, the company said in the filing. The Cloud Migration Services specialization recognizes capabilities in cloud foundation setup, security, and migration. The Infrastructure Modernization Services specialization covers work in building and managing scalable cloud environments, it added.

data.title: Commerzbank on Overnight News
data.description:
Commerzbank in its "European Sunrise" note of Monday highlighted:

Markets: United States Treasuries continue to recover in Asia with a bullish steepening bias. Asian stocks and e-minis are underpinned. EUR tests upside at $1.14. Brent rangebound around $64.5/barrel. Gold slips from new record high.

Fed: Federal Reserve Bank of Boston President Susan Collins says Fed "would absolutely be prepared" to help stabilize markets if conditions become disorderly, while emergency cuts wouldn't be primary tool. New York Federal Reserve President John Williams sees lower growth and inflation up to 3.5%-4% this year with tariffs. Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari says all the Fed can do is to keep inflation expectations anchored.

Tariffs: President Donald Trump's administration exempts smartphones, chips, computers and other electronics from reciprocal tariffs. He later explains that there is no "exception" and 20% tariff applies, products are just moved into a different "bucket"; "nobody is getting off the hook for unfair trade balances." Trump says he'll announce tariffs on semiconductor chips "over the next week."

US: New York Fed gross domestic product Nowcast is little changed at 2.59% for Q1 and improves to 2.62% from 2.44% for Q2. St. Louis Fed model sees 2.9% in Q1.

Argentina reaches $42 billion agreements with the International Monetary Fund ($20 billion), World Bank ($12 billion) and IADB ($10 billion), and central bank eliminates the crawling peg and sets up a managed float within 1,000 to 1,400 pesos/USD range.

==EUROPE:

EU defense: Several European Union finance ministers express interest in idea of a joint defence fund that would buy and own defense equipment, along the lines of Bruegel's EDM proposal.

Germany: Incoming-Prime Minister Friedrich Merz says agreement on cutting income tax is "not fixed," nor is an "automatic" increase in minimum wage to 15 euros in 2026 (BamS). Left party demands a seat in the Expert Commission, which will be tasked to make proposals for the debt brake reform that is planned until year-end.

France will need to find 40 billion euros in savings to reach its 2026 deficit target, Finance Minister Eric Lombard says.

Italy: S&P upgrades Italy to BBB+/stable from BBB/stable, citing improved economic, external, and monetary buffers and gradual progress in stabilising public finances. Finance Minister Giancarlo Giorgetti says Italy has no intention for now of using budget leeway allowed by the EU to boost its defense spending and that there is no need for additional borrowing despite lower growth. The governor of the Bank of Italy Fabio Panetta says Italians needn't worry that creation of digital euro will eliminate physical money.

Ratings: Fitch affirms Spain at A-/positive. Moody's completes reviews of France and Belgium, Scope completes review of the EU without changes. DBRS confirms Lithuania and Malta at A (high)/stable. Scope affirms NRW and Baden-Wuerttemberg at AAA/stable. S&P affirms Hessen at AA+/stable and lowers outlook for Hungary (BBB-) to negative.

==ASIA:

Japan: LDP policy chief says Japan "shouldn't think about intentionally using US Treasury holdings" in negotiations with U.S.

Trade: President Xi Jinping says China and the EU must unite to defend globalization and resist "unilateral acts of bullying" during the visit of Spanish PM Pedro Sanchez in Beijing.

China: Trade surplus rose more than expected in March with exports rising 12.4% and imports falling 4.3%.

data.title: Foremost Clean Energy Gets Nasdaq Notification of Minimum Bid Price Requirement Deficiency
data.description:
Foremost Clean Energy Ltd. (FAT.CN) late on Friday said it received notification from Nasdaq that it is not currently in compliance with the exchange's listing rule stating it must maintain a minimum bid price of US$1 per share.

According to the notification, Foremost's common shares have crossed below the threshold for 31 consecutive business days from Feb. 21 to April 4.

Foremost has been granted a 180-calendar day compliance period, which ends on Oct. 6, to regain compliance. The closing bid price of the company's common shares must meet or exceed the US$1 threshold for a minimum of 10 consecutive business days during this period.

If Foremost does not regain compliance by the deadline, the company may be eligible for an additional 180-calendar day grace period, provided it meets the continued listing requirement for market value of publicly held shares of US$1 million and all other initial listing standards.

The company would also need to notify Nasdaq of its intent to cure the deficiency, which may include implementing a reverse stock split if necessary.

Foremost's management is evaluating options to address the deficiency and remains committed to maintaining its Nasdaq listing.

The company's business operations, financial condition and corporate strategy are unaffected by this development.

data.title: Enlight Raises a Total of $1.5 Billion in Project Finance Following its Third U.S. Financial Close Within Four Months
data.description:
The financial close for Quail Ranch includes $243 million of construction loans; COD is expected towards the end of 2025

Enlight’s three U.S. projects now under construction have a combined capacity of 1.4 FGW and are projected to generate total annual revenues of $135-140 million

TEL AVIV, Israel, April 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Enlight Renewable Energy Ltd. (“Enlight”, “the Company”, NASDAQ: ENLT, TASE: ENLT.TA), a leading global renewable energy platform, announces the financial close for project Quail Ranch (“Quail Ranch” or “the Project”), located near Albuquerque, New Mexico, USA. The Company, through its U.S. subsidiary Clenera Holdings LLC, has secured $243 million in construction financing commitments for the Project.

Combining 128 MW solar generation with 400 MWh of battery storage capacity, Quail Ranch is scheduled for completion towards the end of 2025. Offtake for both generation and storage volumes is secured by a 20-year busbar PPA with the Public Service Company of New Mexico (“PNM”).

The Project is an expansion of Atrisco, which commenced commercial operation in 2024. The shared infrastructure between the two sites accelerated Quail Ranch’s development and will reduce construction and operating costs. Both projects are situated on a desert plateau at an elevation of 1,800 meters, offering optimal solar generation conditions.

Quail Ranch’s financial close joins those of Roadrunner and Country Acres, two other projects now under construction in the U.S., which have achieved a total of $1.5 billion in financing over the past four months with the same consortium of lenders. The three projects have a combined capacity of 1.4 FGW and are expected to generate annual revenues of $135-140 million and EBITDA of $100-110 million when commencing operations in 2025-2026.

The financial close was led by a consortium of four global banks, including BNP Paribas Securities Corp, Crédit Agricole, Natixis Corporate & Investment Banking, and Norddeutsche Landesbank Girozentrale (Nord/LB). Upon the Project’s COD, the construction loan is expected to convert into a $120 million term loan. The Project is expected to be eligible for the Energy Community Tax Credit Bonus, and the Company anticipates finalizing a tax equity transaction during 2025.

Gilad Yavetz, CEO of Enlight, said, “We are proud to have achieved the exceptional milestone of three significant financial closings within such a short timeframe, completing the funding for the second wave of Enlight’s U.S. projects. When operational, they will join Atrisco and Apex to generate combined annual revenues of approximately $200 million in the U.S. Quail Ranch completed its financial close after the administration announced its new tariff policy, demonstrating the project's strength and the Company's preparedness for this scenario.

“Additionally, Enlight is focused on advancing the development of two additional megaprojects in the western U.S. with a combined capacity of 2.6 FGW, and which are located in areas with some of the highest solar irradiation in the country. The new projects are part of the Company's third wave in the U.S., and construction is expected to begin in the coming months.”

“I am very proud to partner with world-leading banks and complete a third major funding package this year,” said Adam Pishl, CEO and President of Clenera. “We continue to demonstrate our ability to bring high-quality projects banks remain excited about, despite market turbulence. Quail Ranch builds on our incredible success in New Mexico and will help meet the high demand for power to fuel American businesses and homes.”

About Enlight Renewable Energy

Founded in 2008, Enlight develops, finances, constructs, owns, and operates utility-scale renewable energy projects. Enlight operates across the three largest renewable segments today: solar, wind and energy storage. A global platform, Enlight operates in the United States, Israel and 10 European countries. Enlight has been traded on the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange since 2010 and completed its U.S. IPO in 2023. Learn more at www.enlightenergy.co.il.

data.title: Uranium Holds Near 18-Month Low
data.description:
Uranium futures were steady around $64 per pound in April, the lowest in 18 months, amid the continued backdrop of sufficient supply and uncertain demand.

US President Trump did not include Russia in its major reciprocal tariffs to underscore earlier signals that he aims to secure economic ties with the Russia, ramping up bets that Washington may remove current sanctions against the import of Russian nuclear fuel.

The latest estimates indicated that Russia hosted nearly half of the global uranium conversion and enrichment capacity, against a backdrop of plentiful supply of yellowcake fore Western enrichers with capacity constraints.

In the meantime, markets reconsidered their speculative positions on nuclear power demand for US datacenters following the emergence of more efficient large language models in Europe and China.

Consistently, Microsoft reportedly canceled leases on new datacenters, marking a contrast with the view that tech giants were racing to secure new power capacity.

data.title: Unicredit's Stake Increase in Commerzbank Cleared by German Antitrust Watchdog
data.description:
(09:25 GMT) *UniCredit Receives Approval From German Federal Cartel Office to Lift Stake in Commerzbank to 29.9%
(09:28 GMT) *German Federal Cartel Office: Inspection Didn't Find Any Merger-Related Problems Due to Diverse Provider Structure
(09:29 GMT) *German Federal Cartel Office Conducted Extensive Investigations Into Major Competitors to Examine Provider Structures
(09:30 GMT) *German Federal Cartel Office Held Discussions With KfW, SME Associations Representing the Interests of Both Lenders
(09:32 GMT) *German Federal Cartel Office: Relevant Alternatives Will Remain for Customers After UniCredit, Commerzbank Merger
(10:09 GMT) Unicredit's Stake Increase in Commerzbank Cleared by German Antitrust Watchdog

By Helena Smolak

Germany's Federal Cartel Office approved UniCredit's plans to increase its stake in Commerzbank to just under 30%, bringing the Italian lender a step closer to a potential takeover of its German rival.

The approval follows a investigation by the regulator, in which it said it found no competition concerns due to what it called the diverse provider structure in the German banking market.

"The notified minority acquisition alone will strengthen Unicredit's market position in the private and corporate customer business in Germany," said Andreas Mundt, president of the German Federal Cartel Office. "We have therefore taken a close look at the financial services particularly affected. Other significant competitors are active in all areas, which is why the transaction had to be cleared."

Italian lender UniCredit, which aims to build a pan-European banking champion, received a green light for the stake increase from the European Central Bank last month.

The national competition authority's investigation included inspections of major competitors to examine provider structures, as well as discussions with German state-owned investment and development bank KfW and small-to-medium enterprise associations representing the interests of both lenders.

Unicredit began acquiring shares in Commerzbank last year and has so far secured a 28% interest in Commerzbank, partly through financial contracts.

According to German law, a 30% shareholding would trigger a mandatory takeover bid. Unicredit Chief Executive Andrea Orcel said he would take his time to decide whether or not the bank will proceed with a possible merger.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com
